codeReview.summary: The reasoning-omission fix is sound, but the filtered-history work is incomplete: the runner only snapshots call_model_input_filter rewrites on the first turn, so multi-turn filtered continuations are lost from normalized replay/durable state. The feature also does not add the structured-output max_turn regression coverage required by VAL-CORE-009.

issues.description: The new filtered-history bookkeeping only records `model_data.input` when `normalized_generated_items.is_empty()`. Any `call_model_input_filter` rewrite on turn 2+ is therefore dropped from `run_state.normalized_input`/`RunResult.normalized_input`, while `normalized_new_items` still replays the unfiltered tool-call history. A realistic trigger is a per-turn filter such as tool-output trimming or a filter that removes prior tool calls after the first tool loop: the model sees the filtered continuation, but `to_input_list_mode(Normalized)` and durable resume replay the unfiltered history instead. Upstream tracks the full filtered continuation after every turn (`reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/run_internal/run_loop.py:937-944`), so this does not yet match parity.

VAL-CORE-009 requires max-turn exhaustion parity for plain, streamed, and structured-output runs, but the only new regression here exercises a plain function-tool loop. I could not find any Rust assertion covering structured-output max-turn exhaustion, while the pinned upstream suite has dedicated non-streamed and streamed structured-output cases (`reference/openai-agents-python/tests/test_max_turns.py:97-140`). As written, this feature does not fully close the contracted max-turn parity surface it claims to fulfill.

summary: Fail: multi-turn `call_model_input_filter` rewrites are still lost from normalized replay state, and the feature did not add the structured-output `max_turns` coverage required by the validation contract. Report written for commit e3962583a1e16ffb80b5d5a2f2df995dcc5539ca.
